summary: The actual screenshot shows a detailed planet zoom window with colored continents and labels, overlaid on a gameplay map, but deviates from the wireframe in layout, text, and detail level. The wireframe provides a basic outline but is insufficient for detailed validation of the screen's functional requirements.

correctElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: Dark starfield/game-map background
correctElements.evidence: Both actual and wireframe feature a dark background with starfield elements.

correctElements.requirement: Screen description: Window contains header text and a large circular planet surface
correctElements.evidence: Central window present in both with header and circular planet representation.

correctElements.requirement: FR-UI-SCREEN-CONTINENT-ZOOM-WINDOW: Visually satisfy documented screen description
correctElements.evidence: Top resource indicators (POP, MET, FUEL) are present and similarly positioned.

correctElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: Terminal-style labels
correctElements.evidence: Map Key panel on the right with labels matches style in both.

incorrectElements.requirement: FR-UI-SCREEN-CONTINENT-ZOOM-WINDOW: Visually satisfy expected user-facing UI behavior
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Phase text differs, which may indicate a mismatch in game state or incorrect rendering for the screen.
Title includes specific planet name in actual, but wireframe has generic title, affecting specificity validation.

incorrectElements.requirement: Screen description: Large XNA-hosted circular planet surface with continent boundaries and labels
incorrectElements.actual: Colored semicircle with specific continent names (Lankhmar, Rillanon, etc.) and boundaries
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Actual has detailed, colored, labeled continents not represented in wireframe's simplistic division, missing key visual elements for comparison.

incorrectElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: Fixed screen-relative geometry
incorrectElements.actual: Planets labeled 'Sirius', 'Canopus' with different positions and additional elements like 'Sirius b'
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Extra labels and mismatched positions/representations violate fixed geometry; wireframe lacks detail on dynamic elements.

incorrectElements.expected: No close button shown in wireframe
incorrectElements.actual: Close button present at bottom of window
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Missing in wireframe, preventing validation of interactive elements.

incorrectElements.requirement: TR-UI-AUDIT-HWND-001: Actual screenshot from RiskyStars client HWND
incorrectElements.actual: Longer debug string with more panels info
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Difference in debug output may indicate runtime variations not captured in wireframe.

incorrectElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: Thin green panel borders, fixed geometry at 1536x832
incorrectElements.expected: Central window spanning most of the height with green borders
incorrectElements.actual: Central window is shorter, with additional space below and different border nesting
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Bounds and nesting differ slightly due to DPI or scaling, though resolution matches; residual risk of pixel-perfect mismatch in automated tests.

wireframeSuitability.why: The wireframe captures the basic layout and style but lacks detail on the continent boundaries, labels, and colors, making it inadequate for fully validating the XNA-hosted planet surface and dynamic elements as per the screen description and FR-UI-SCREEN-CONTINENT-ZOOM-WINDOW.
missingRequirements: Detailed representation of continent boundaries and player-colored regions (from screen description)
Interactive elements like Close button (implied in user-facing behavior FR-UI-SCREEN-CONTINENT-ZOOM-WINDOW)
Validation of repository process requirements from AGENTS-README-FIRST.yaml, such as session logging integration (TR-UI-AUDIT-AGENTS-001)
recommendedWireframeChanges: Add detailed continent divisions with example labels and colors to match screen description
Include interactive controls like Close button
Incorporate placeholders for dynamic debug text and game state variations
Ensure wireframe includes elements for process validation, e.g., markers for MCP integration if relevant
